messages.content:
Listen to Mia, Tom and Owen answering the following questions. Write Mia, Tom or Owen next to the question they answer.

1 How has your country changed in the last thirty years?  answer
 
2 Is the world getting better or worse? answer
 
3 How has your life changed in the last thirty years? answer

1. Tom
2. Owen
3. Mia

Choose the correct variant.
Tom says that Beijing has grown because people

Выберите один ответ:

came from all over China to make money


don't have as much money as before


came from around the world to get rich
came from all over China to make money

Choose the correct variant.
Mia thinks that thirty years ago

life was simpler

the pace of life was faster

people were happier
life was simpler

Choose the correct variant.
Tom says that life in the countryside

has not changed that much, but people have more technology

has changed enormously because of new technology

will never change because they do not have access to technology
has not changed that much, but people have more technology

Complete the questions for the answers.


Q: At -30°C, when will a small *answer*?
A: Almost immediately.

Q: At -30°C, when will a small object freeze?
A: Almost immediately.

It depends where you are in Russia and what the temperature is at the time. But, potentially, yes. At a temperature of -30°C, small amounts of water will turn into ice almost immediately.	Immanuel Kanevsky

Complete the questions for the answers.

Q: At -30°C, when will a small answer?
A: Almost immediately.

Q: At what temperature will small amounts of water turn into ice almost immediately?
A: -30°C.

Read the text.
It depends where you are in Russia and what the temperature is at the time. But, potentially, yes. At a temperature of -30°C, small amounts of water will turn into ice almost immediately.	Immanuel Kanevsky

Complete the questions for the answers.

Q: At -30°C, when will a small *answer*?
A: Almost immediately.

*answer*?
At a temperature of -30°C, when will small amounts of water turn into ice?

Complete the questions for the answers.


Q: Who thought sea air was *answer*?
A: The Victorians.

Q: According to whom was sea air beneficial?
A: The Victorians.

Find the word in the text to match the given meaning.

remove dangerous chemicals or poison from something (paragraph 1)
decontaminate

paragraph 1: You are going to stay on a desert island for several months and you can only choose three foods to take with you. Which do you choose, and why?

Broccoli, walnuts and orange juice. Broccoli has a chemical which helps detoxify your liver. It's also a superfood. Walnuts have protein and plenty of healthy fats. Orange juice is a source of clean water, and the orange contains Vitamin C.	Dr Leah Morecombe

Find the word in the text to match the given meaning.

remove dangerous chemicals or poison from something (paragraph 1)
detoxify

paragraph 2: What's worse for you: boredom or stress?

Boredom. Stress can have benefits. Weightlifting is a type of stress. So are other sports and pressures at work. All of these are good for you in small doses. Boredom means you have no purpose in life, and no dose of boredom is good for you. Dr Samran Naipaul

Find the word in the text to match the given meaning.

measured amount of something that you experience at one time (paragraph 2)
dose

Complete the sentences with the correct comparative or superlative form of the adjective in brackets.

His marks in the last test were slightly *answer* in maths, but much *answer* in English. (low / high)
His marks in the last test were slightly lower in maths, but much higher in English.

Complete the sentences with the correct comparative or superlative form of the adjective in brackets.

It’s far *answer* to fly by plane these days and it is also one of *answer* ways to travel. (cheap / safe)
It’s far cheaper to fly by plane these days and it is also one of the safest ways to travel.

Complete the sentences with the correct comparative or superlative form of the adjective in brackets.

Our new car is a little bit *answer* than the last one and is also a lot *answer* to drive. (economical / easy)
Our new car is a little bit more economical than the last one and is also a lot easier to drive.

Write the correct question tag.

Susan and Michael aren’t coming to the wedding, answer?
are they?

Write the correct question tag.

Zhen’s already finished university, answer?
hasn't she?

Write the correct question tag.

Richard wasn’t working in South Africa then, answer?
was he?

Write the correct question tag.

Margaret hasn’t passed her driving test, answer?
has she?

Give the word to the definition.

a form of enriched multimedia which allows an interactive and dynamic linking of visual and audio elements
Hypermedia.

Translate into English.

звуковая карта
sound card

Translate into English.

вебкаст
webcast

Give the word to the definition.

text with links which take you to other pages
Hyperlink.

Translate into English.

плагин
plugin
